meaning: n.消耗品,可牺牲的,可花费的,可消耗的
examples: During the recession, training budgets were seen as an expendable luxury.
And innocent people's lives are not expendable.
Because batteries are expendable, power management is critical.
The expendable Vernon Wells gets traded to New York -- where he's hotter than hot.
The notion that in some ways we would treat them as expendable makes no sense.
